Course Title: Targeting Critical Resources with AI-Driven Attacks
Category: Agriculture & Resources: Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing, Mining, Energy
Who is this for: Operations managers, plant supervisors, facility managers, control room operators, maintenance technicians, field engineers, agricultural managers, mining supervisors, energy sector workers, infrastructure specialists, safety coordinators
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Engineering, Facilities Management, Safety & Security, Risk Management, Information Technology, Operational Technology
Description: How attackers may exploit AI systems in energy, mining, and agriculture.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how critical infrastructure sectors such as energy, mining, and agriculture operate, along with a general awareness of cybersecurity concepts, will help you get the most from this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key vulnerabilities in AI-driven systems across energy, mining, and agriculture sectors, and explain how attackers may exploit these weaknesses to disrupt critical resources. You will also apply foundational security awareness principles to recognise early warning signs of AI-targeted attacks within resource-based industries, helping you contribute to more resilient operational practices.
